page: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 processors. The system features 192 physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and the Nitro hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 75 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m8a.48xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, as well as Passmark CPU and memory benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached read-only small block speeds and uncached large block read/write speeds registering in the top 10%. Cryptographic and compression workloads, including gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, also achieve top-tier throughput.

For advanced workloads, the instance shows strong capabilities in large language model inference. Prompt processing across small, medium, and large models ranks in the top 10%, while text generation for medium and large models falls within the top 25%. However, text generation for small models is limited to average tier performance. The hardware supports advanced vector and cryptographic instruction sets, including AVX-512 and VAES, which assist in accelerating database operations and static web serving.

The instance offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale deployments that require substantial memory bandwidth and compute power without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU accelerators, making it reliant on network storage. It is positioned for workloads such as large-scale database operations, static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 6 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a 75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, it delivers top-tier performance in multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. It also achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds for large language models, though text generation for smaller models remains in the average tier. This instance is designed for memory-intensive general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and CPU-based inference tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 75 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier compute and memory benchmark performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.48xlarge features 192 AMD EPYC cores and 768 GB DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier compute and memory benchmark results.
tagline: A high-density general-purpose platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, rapid DDR5 memory, and high-bandwidth network connectivity for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 cores operating at 4.5 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
75 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by Nitro hypervisor
Top-tier performance in database operations and memory bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Designed for software compilation, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
